ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-02-2011 Continued To May-23-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Roland Fisher for Petitioner.

The accounting petition was continued to May 23, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the purpose of Mr. Fisher obtaining signed consents from Victor and Carlos. Mr. Fisher is to notify the Examiner of the date of death of Luis as soon as he obtains that information. Mr. Fisher also spoke of the Family relationship, living arrangements for Victor and Carlos and Ms. Gordillo and her Brother acting as Constructive Trustees.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Closing The Estate
Off Calendar Mar-16-2010 Continued To Sep-28-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Fisher for Rosabel Gordillo.

Mr. Fisher stated that he had never met his Client, that Ms. Gordillo was a Co-Petitioner/Co-Executor with her Brother who had expired, that this was an unusual will that created a Trust under the will itself allowing the Brothers (2) to live in the house as long as they paid rent and that April 16, 2010 was the ten year anniversary which would give them no further right to remain in the house. In addition, he stated that house was listed, that the Broker was unable to move the property, that the house was in bad shape, that they would have more leverage come April 16, 2010 and that there was a Brother who was developmentally disabled who resides in the East Bay. Furthermore, Mr. Fisher stated that a Trust would have to be created for the Brother living in the East Bay, that listing of the property, that removal of the Brothers, that sale of the property and arranging of distribution to the Brother in the East Bay needed to be done. The matter was continued to September 28, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of closing of the estate.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
